title: Artan to referee Uefa Super Cup after losing World Cup role
content: Somali referee Omar Artan is appointed to take charge of the Uefa Super Cup match between Paris St-Germain and Aston Villa later this summer after being denied a spot at the World Cup.

content: Privacy watchdog finds xAI's Grok lacks safeguards for sexualised deepfake image sharing, amid growing global scrutiny.

content: The proposal, if passed, would mark one of the largest expansions of illegal Israeli settlements in decades.

originalTitle: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
content: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

originalTitle: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
title: A Sad Day in Sudan: Let's Talk About Peace
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's me, Aisha, your kid-friendly reporter from right here in our incredible continent, Africa! I hope you're all having a wonderful day, playing, learning, and sharing smiles. Today, we need to talk about some news from a country called Sudan. Sudan is a very special place, just like where you live, with its own beautiful stories, amazing animals, and kind people. It's a country in the northeast part of Africa, and it's super big!

A Little Bit About Sudan

Imagine a country with big deserts, green lands along a giant river called the Nile, and ancient pyramids! That's Sudan. It's a place rich with history and culture. Many different people live there, speaking different languages and sharing wonderful traditions. Just like your town or village, Sudan is full of families, schools, and playgrounds. Everyone wants to live safely and happily, don't they? **Did you know?** Sudan is home to more ancient pyramids than Egypt! Isn't that amazing? History is everywhere in Africa!

A Sad Day in el-Obeid

Now, for the sad news. In a city in Sudan called el-Obeid, something very difficult happened. Imagine a day when people were walking together, being very quiet and sad. They were going to a special gathering called a funeral. A funeral is when family and friends come together to say goodbye to someone they loved who has passed away. It’s a time to remember happy memories and comfort each other. On this very sad day, while these people were walking peacefully, a flying robot machine, called a drone, caused a lot of trouble. Some people got hurt, and it made everyone very scared and upset.

What is a Funeral Procession?

Let's talk a little more about what happened. When someone we love dies, it’s a very sad time. A funeral is a ceremony where people come together to remember that person. Sometimes, everyone walks together in a line, slowly and respectfully, to a special place. This walk is called a funeral procession. It’s a quiet and important time for families to be together and say goodbye. It’s a time when everyone should feel safe and peaceful.

What is a Drone?

You might have seen pictures of drones. A drone is like a small flying robot. Some drones are toys that people fly for fun. But other drones are bigger and can be used for different things. Some help deliver medicines to far-away places, which is good! But sadly, some drones are used by groups of people to drop things or make loud noises in places where there is fighting. On this sad day in el-Obeid, a drone was used in a way that hurt people, which is never okay. **Did you know?** Drones can also be used to help! They can deliver important packages, take amazing photos from high up, or even help farmers check their crops!

Why Was There Fighting?

You might be wondering why this happened. Sometimes, in a country, different groups of grown-ups don't agree on how the country should be run. When they can't agree, and they have different ideas, sometimes they start to fight. This kind of fighting inside one country is sometimes called a "civil war." In Sudan, there are two big groups of fighters. They have been fighting each other for some time now. El-Obeid is a city where this fighting has been happening a lot, which makes it a very difficult place for families to live.

Who is Helping?

Even when sad things happen, there are always good people trying to help. There are groups called "rights groups." These are like grown-ups who are super good at making sure everyone is treated fairly and safely. They speak up for people who are hurt or who are in danger. They are like kind heroes who remind everyone that people, especially children and families, should always be safe and protected, even when there is fighting. They are saying that what happened in el-Obeid was very wrong because regular people, not soldiers, were hurt. **Did you know?** There are many amazing people all over Africa and the world who work every day to help others, just like these "rights groups"! They are called humanitarians, and they help make sure everyone gets food, water, and safety.

The Importance of Peace

What everyone wants, especially the children and families in Sudan, is peace. Peace means everyone can live without fear. It means children can go to school, play with their friends, and sleep soundly at night. It means families can be together and grow food and build their homes without worrying about fighting. Grown-ups all over the world are trying to help the groups in Sudan stop fighting and find a peaceful way to live together. It's not easy, but it's very important.

Working Towards a Brighter Tomorrow

Even though this news is sad, we must always remember that there is hope. Many people are praying for Sudan, and many grown-ups are working very hard to bring peace back to the country. They are talking to the groups who are fighting and trying to help them understand that hurting people is never the answer. Just like we learn to share and be kind to our friends, grown-ups need to learn how to share and be kind to each other too, especially when they disagree.

A Message for Our Friends in Sudan

To all our friends in Sudan, especially the children: know that people care about you. We hope that peace will come to your beautiful country very soon. We believe that with kindness, talking, and helping each other, even the biggest problems can be solved. Let's all wish for peace, not just in Sudan, but all over our amazing continent and the world. Remember to be kind to each other every day, because kindness is a superpower that can change the world! **Did you know?** The word "Ubuntu" from Southern Africa means "I am because we are." It teaches us that we are all connected and should care for each other! Thank you for listening, my friends. Let’s keep sending our good wishes and thoughts for peace to Sudan. This is Aisha, your reporter, signing off for now. See you next time!
summary: In Sudan, a flying robot plane hurt people at a funeral, and now kind people are working to stop the fighting and bring peace.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how fighting between grown-ups can hurt innocent people, even at sad times like funerals, and reminds us that everyone deserves to live in a safe and peaceful home.
